task_completion.description: Run Lighthouse audit on Khan Academy
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser open to khanacademy.org with the DevTools Lighthouse panel displaying scores for Performance and Accessibility.
Browser open to coursera.org with the DevTools Lighthouse panel displaying scores for Performance and Accessibility.

task_completion.description: Run Lighthouse audit on MIT OpenCourseWare
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser open to ocw.mit.edu with the DevTools Lighthouse panel displaying scores for Performance and Accessibility.

task_completion.description: Create the audit report file at the specified desktop path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing a file being created or saved specifically at /home/ga/Desktop/lighthouse_audit_report.txt.

task_completion.description: Report includes names, URLs, and Lighthouse scores for all three sites
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the text file clearly list Khan Academy, Coursera, and MIT OpenCourseWare, their respective URLs, and specific numeric scores for Performance and Accessibility.

task_completion.description: Report includes a recommendation on technical suitability
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains a concluding paragraph or sentence recommending whether the sites are technically suitable for adoption based on the audit scores.

integrity.description: Agent used a real web browser and DevTools to run the audits
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show a real browser (like Chromium/Edge) with the DevTools Lighthouse tab open and running, not just terminal commands or text-based scraping.

integrity.description: Scores in the report match the actual audit results
integrity.visual_evidence: The Performance and Accessibility numbers typed into the report should correspond to the numbers visible in the Lighthouse audit screenshots.

integrity.description: Agent did not copy-paste a pre-written report or hallucinate data without auditing
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be a clear chronological progression: opening sites, running audits, then writing the report. The report should not appear instantly without prior browser interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target sites are Khan Academy (khanacademy.org), Coursera (coursera.org), and MIT OpenCourseWare (ocw.mit.edu). The required Lighthouse audit categories are 'Performance' and 'Accessibility', which are standard metrics in Chromium-based browser DevTools. The final report must be located at /home/ga/Desktop/lighthouse_audit_report.txt.
